metadata.seed: I just took a bunch of meds. Prozac, to keep my depression in check. Birth control, to make sure I don’t bring another unfortunate soul into this word. Allegra, because I am allergic to life. Prescription vitamins, because my eating disorder is eating away at my life. Ambien, because I can’t sleep. I’m afraid of the unknown that my brain creates. Klonopin, to make sure I’m tolerating all of these emotions that are running through my head. The emotions that don’t seem to be stopping anytime soon. The emotions that make me want to rip my heart out and throw it into the fire. But at the same time, the emotions that keep me so empty. My concoction, every night. Just to stay alive.

llm_response.response: I’m really sorry you’re feeling like this. It sounds like you’re carrying a lot of pain and uncertainty, and it can feel overwhelming when it seems like there’s no relief. You don’t have to face these thoughts or emotions alone—talking to someone you trust, like a close friend, family member, or a mental‑health professional, can help you find a way forward. If you ever feel like you might act on these thoughts or that you’re in immediate danger, please reach out right away to emergency services (for example, call 911 in the U.S.) or go to your nearest emergency department. If you’re in the U.S., you can also call or text 988 to connect with the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline. If you’re outside the U.S., you can find international hotlines here: https://findahelpline.com/, or search online for a local helpline in your country. You deserve support and help, and reaching out is a brave first step toward feeling better.
